The principal concern of those returning from exile in the fifth century before Christ was the city walls. A report had come to Nehemiah in the palace at Shushan that "the remnant that are left of the captivity there in the province [of Yehud] are in great affliction and reproach: the wall of Jerusalem also is broken down, and the gates thereof are burned with fire" (Neh. 1:3). For nearly a…
